subject: Sports
title: Concussion Science in Contact Sports
description: A quick, practical guide to Concussion Science in Contact Sports.

hook.text: The brain floats in fluid inside the skull. A hard hit makes it slam against bone. That's a concussion.

buildup.text: Concussions cause chemical changes in the brain—confusion, dizziness, memory loss, and sometimes lasting damage.

discovery.text: CTE—chronic traumatic encephalopathy—was found in the brains of deceased NFL players. Repeated hits accumulate.

twist.text: Dr. Bennet Omalu discovered CTE in 2002. The NFL initially denied the link for years before acknowledging it.

climax.text: Rule changes, better helmets, and concussion protocols now aim to protect athletes from invisible brain injuries.

punchline.text: The hardest hits leave no visible bruise but can change a brain forever.

quiz.question: What is CTE?
options: A brain disease caused by repeated head impacts
